reasoning: Candidate RIGHT: the echo button frame itself uses warm parchment/tan tones with a golden pixel border — that part is on-tone. However the central icon is a simplified face with two large hollow dark oval eye sockets and an open lower-oval mouth, which reads unmistakably as a skull or death-mask rather than a cute/charming fairy-tale character. In Nintendo family-friendly contexts a ghost face can be cute (Boo), but execution here lacks the round cheeks, bright eyes, and expressive warmth needed — the hollow sockets are more occult-memento-mori than whimsical. Peer reference LEFT is a severe tone violation on its own: the spritesheet contains stone-dungeon tiles, blood-red splatters, dark gore pools, fire burn effects, and desaturated dungeon-crawler textures — this entire reference cluster is OUT OF TARGET. The candidate inherits that visual DNA. Taken together, the asset does not meet the bright/warm/sunny Hyrule / toy-diorama / wholesome Nintendo standard.
